Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search Slack (#operations) or Email for "Tyler Okonkwo" to find his relocation details (Philadelphia → Denver, must vacate by April 18, Denver lease starts May 1, creating a 13-day or Apr 18 - May 1 storage gap).
Search Slack (#operations) or Airtable to find Tyler's storage vendor arrangement (Chloe shifted dates by one day due to coordinator conflict).
Search Slack (#finance) or Email to find the Canopy surcharge details (Hana posted $9,210 total, including $2,650 pending client approval).
Search Email, Slack, or CRM to check if Patricia Langford (Canopy HR) has approved the pending surcharges.
Search Slack (#operations) or Airtable for "Keiko Tanaka" to find her relocation details (Columbus → Seattle, 400-lb laser cutter, DOT hazmat required, 8-day lease gap).
Search Slack (#operations or #finance) or Email to investigate the DOT compliance audit regarding the laser cutter.
Discover that Blessing Okafor was the coordinator who failed to file the DOT placarding certificate for the laser cutter.
Discover that Greg Pallone from Swift Relocations provided the signed placarding certificate retroactively.
Verify the status of Keiko Tanaka's 8-day lease gap interim storage arrangement.
Update Tyler Okonkwo's Airtable relocation record with his 13-day (or Apr 18 - May 1) storage status and the pending Canopy surcharge approval status (waiting on Patricia Langford).
Update Keiko Tanaka's Airtable relocation record with her 8-day storage gap status and corrected DOT documentation status (obtained from Swift/Greg Pallone).
Create Linear tracking ticket(s) for the unresolved Canopy surcharge approval ($2,650 pending total), Keiko Tanaka's 8-day lease gap storage, and Tyler Okonkwo's unconfirmed vendor rate/extended-stay surcharge for his storage.
Post a complete status summary in the #operations Slack channel detailing the findings for both moves, including who dropped the DOT cert and confirming it was recovered.
